arts.ttl: Exeter City have to be brave against 'best team in League One'
arts.sum: MANAGER Gary Caldwell has called on his players to be brave and do what they do best when Exeter City play host to “the best team in League One” at St James Park tonight. They’re by far the best possession-based team in this league and their rotations and how they try and play the game is quite similar to the set-up that we have, definitely in possession.

arts.ttl: I watched the Shrewsbury Town defeat and Birmingham City will be kicking themselves
arts.sum: Caldwell has watched video of the game on Saturday, which Blues lost 3-2 - only their second defeat of the campaign but their third game in the league without a win - and has determined that everything fell into place on the day for Shrewsbury, who were benefitting from the injection of enthusiasm courtesy of new manager Gareth Ainsworth. Exeter City boss Gary Caldwell believes that if Birmingham City played Shrewsbury Town 10 times on the spin, based on what he saw and the patterns of play from Saturday's game, Blues would win on at least nine occasions.

arts.ttl: Wrexham and Birmingham City fans are being 'scalped' by costly away tickets, claims expert
arts.sum: Football finance expert Kieran Maguire believes a price cap is needed for away tickets in the EFL

arts.ttl: Wrexham and Birmingham City tipped for promotion and to spend 'obscene' money
arts.sum: Wycombe's Jack Grimmer believes Wrexham and Birmingham will spend lots of money in the January transfer window

arts.ttl: Jay Stansfield set to make emotional return to St James Park against Exeter City
arts.sum: Stanno enjoyed a sentimental return to Exeter on a season-long loan from Fulham in the 2022-23 season, scoring nine goals for the club, and signed off with a stunning hat-trick in the season-ending 3-2 win over Morecambe at St James Park. JAY Stansfield will make an emotional return to St James Park with his Birmingham City team tomorrow night but Exeter City manager Gary Caldwell is in no mood to do him any favours.

arts.ttl: Exeter City Women crush Plymouth Argyle with five-star thrashing
arts.sum: Goals from Mollie Taylor, Sophie Gillies, Sarah Stacey (2), and Georgie Barbour-Gresham capped off a commanding display from the Grecians as they progressed to the third round of the Adobe Women's FA Cup. Exeter City Women will host Worthing FC in the third round of the Adobe Women's FA Cup after the Grecians delivered a five-star display in the Devon Derby, cruising to a 5-1 victory over Plymouth Argyle Women.
